title: Authors and Citation

title: Authors and Citation
text: Kenny C (2026). wf: Artificial Intelligence Workflow Tools. R package version 0.1.0, https://christophertkenny.com/wf/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {wf: Artificial Intelligence Workflow Tools},   author = {Christopher T. Kenny},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://christophertkenny.com/wf/}, }

title: Artificial Intelligence Workflow Tools
text: wf manages building blocks AI coding agents: skills, agents, rules, hooks. type lives conventional directory (e.g. .claude/skills/ Claude Code skills) can installed GitHub local path, tracked updated lock file.

title: Artificial Intelligence Workflow Tools
text: install stable version CRAN: install development version:
code: install.package('wf') # install.packages('pak') pak::pak('christopherkenny/wf')

title: Artificial Intelligence Workflow Tools
text: Set WF_AGENT .Renviron every wf function knows agent use: Add line like: Supported agents: 'claude_code' (alias: 'claude'), 'openclaw', 'codex', 'cursor', 'gemini_cli', 'github_copilot' (alias: 'copilot'), 'posit_ai' (alias: 'posit'). type *_path() function looking conventional directory: path functions also accept scope = 'global' user-wide installation, auto-detect agent WF_AGENT called without arguments.
code: usethis::edit_r_environ() WF_AGENT='claude_code' library(wf)  skill_path('claude_code', 'project') #> [1] ".claude/skills" agent_path('claude_code', 'project') #> [1] ".claude/agents" rule_path('claude_code', 'project') #> [1] ".claude/rules" hook_path('claude_code', 'project') #> [1] ".claude/hooks"

title: Artificial Intelligence Workflow Tools
text: Install skill GitHub using owner/repo shorthand full URL: repos bundle multiple skills skills/ subdirectory, use skill argument: List, check, update installed skills: Search GitHub community skills scaffold new one:
code: add_skill('some-user/some-skill') add_skill('some-user/skills', skill = 'proofread') list_skills() #>                                                           name #> .claude/skills/cran-extrachecks               cran-extrachecks #> .claude/skills/tidy-argument-checking              types-check #> .claude/skills/tidy-deprecate-function tidy-deprecate-function #>                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         description #> .claude/skills/cran-extrachecks        Prepare R packages for CRAN submission by checking for common ad-hoc requirements not caught by devtools::check(). Use when: (1) Preparing a package for first CRAN release, (2) Preparing a package update for CRAN resubmission, (3) Reviewing a package to ensure CRAN compliance, (4) Responding to CRAN reviewer feedback. Covers documentation requirements, DESCRIPTION field standards, URL validation, examples, and administrative requirements.\n #> .claude/skills/tidy-argument-checking                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Validate function inputs in R using a standalone file of check_* functions. Use when writing exported R functions that need input validation, reviewing existing validation code, or when creating new input validation helpers. #> .claude/skills/tidy-deprecate-function                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                Guide for deprecating R functions/arguments. Use when a user asks to deprecate a function or parameter, including adding lifecycle warnings, updating documentation, adding NEWS entries, and updating tests. #>                                                                                                      source #> .claude/skills/cran-extrachecks        https://github.com/posit-dev/skills/tree/HEAD/r-lib/cran-extrachecks #> .claude/skills/tidy-argument-checking                                                                  <NA> #> .claude/skills/tidy-deprecate-function                                                                 <NA> #>                                                installed_at #> .claude/skills/cran-extrachecks        2026-03-11T13:56:50Z #> .claude/skills/tidy-argument-checking                  <NA> #> .claude/skills/tidy-deprecate-function                 <NA> check_skills() #>               name                            installed_sha #> 1 cran-extrachecks bf0fc0d480209a2f5f7fdf32eabb9c7546e53ee5 #>                                 latest_sha update_available #> 1 bf0fc0d480209a2f5f7fdf32eabb9c7546e53ee5            FALSE update_skills() #> All skills are up to date. find_skill('quarto') #>     name                                  description            owner #> 1 skills [WIP] Claude Skills for Political Scientists christopherkenny #>                                          url stars #> 1 https://github.com/christopherkenny/skills     3  tmp <- tempfile() init_skill('my-skill', tmp) #> Created skill "my-skill" at #> 'C:/Users/chris/AppData/Local/Temp/RtmpScozzt/file39c824a4155b/my-skill'.

title: Artificial Intelligence Workflow Tools
text: Custom subagents single .md files YAML frontmatter. Install one GitHub: multi-agent repos, use agent argument: Create local template get started:
code: add_agent('some-user/some-agent') add_agent('some-user/agents', agent = 'code-reviewer') tmp <- tempfile() init_agent('my-agent', tmp) #> Created agent "my-agent" at #> 'C:/Users/chris/AppData/Local/Temp/RtmpScozzt/file39c84f5d7173/my-agent.md'.

title: Artificial Intelligence Workflow Tools
text: Rules Markdown files set coding guidelines every session. Install GitHub: Create local template:
code: add_rule('some-user/some-rule') tmp <- tempfile() init_rule('my-rule', tmp) #> Created rule "my-rule" at #> 'C:/Users/chris/AppData/Local/Temp/RtmpScozzt/file39c8416e70da/my-rule.md'.

title: Artificial Intelligence Workflow Tools
text: Hooks executable scripts (.sh, .R, etc.) run agent lifecycle events. Installing hook copies script .claude/hooks/ registers settings.json. register bare command without installing script file: List registered hooks create new script template:
code: add_hook('some-user/some-hook', event = 'PreToolUse') register_hook('PreToolUse', 'echo hello') list_hooks() #> [1] event   matcher command file    #> <0 rows> (or 0-length row.names)  tmp <- tempfile() init_hook('my-hook', tmp) #> Created hook "my-hook" at #> 'C:/Users/chris/AppData/Local/Temp/RtmpScozzt/file39c866845bcc/my-hook.sh'. #> ℹ Register it with `register_hook(event, command = #>   "C:/Users/chris/AppData/Local/Temp/RtmpScozzt/file39c866845bcc/my-hook.sh")`

title: Install an agent — add_agent
text: Installs custom agent GitHub repository local file agents directory. Agents single Markdown files YAML frontmatter specifying minimum name description.

title: Install an agent — add_agent
code: add_agent(source, agent = NULL, path = NULL, overwrite = FALSE)

title: Install an agent — add_agent
text: source One : GitHub URL pointing repo, e.g. "https://github.com/owner/repo". GitHub URL pointing subdirectory file, e.g. "https://github.com/owner/repo/tree/main/path//agent.md". GitHub shorthand, e.g. "owner/repo". local file path pointing Markdown file. agent multi-agent repositories store agents agents/ subdirectory, name agent install (without .md extension), e.g. agent = "code-reviewer". supplied, agent read agents/<agent>.md within repository. Ignored source already points specific path via /tree/.... path agents directory. Can one : known coding agent name 'claude_code', 'cursor', 'github_copilot' (see agent_path() full list) use agent's conventional project-scope path. character string giving directory path directly. NULL (default), case path resolved WF_AGENT environment variable, prompting interactive sessions. Set WF_AGENT .Renviron (e.g. usethis::edit_r_environ()) avoid prompt. overwrite FALSE (default), error raised agent already installed. Set TRUE replace .

title: Install an agent — add_agent
text: path installed agent file, invisibly.

title: Install an agent — add_agent
code: src <- tempfile(fileext = '.md') writeLines(   c('---', 'name: example', 'description: An example agent.', '---'),   src ) add_agent(src, path = tempfile()) #> Installed agent "example" to /tmp/Rtmp1p0v0x/file1be53fa03cd7/example.md.

title: Install a hook script — add_hook
text: Installs hook script GitHub repository local file hooks directory, registers corresponding settings.json runs specified lifecycle event.

title: Install a hook script — add_hook
code: add_hook(   source,   event,   hook = NULL,   matcher = NULL,   path = NULL,   settings = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   timeout = NULL,   async = FALSE )

title: Install a hook script — add_hook
text: source One : GitHub URL pointing repo, e.g. "https://github.com/owner/repo". GitHub shorthand, e.g. "owner/repo". local file path pointing executable script (.sh, .R, .py, etc.). event lifecycle event attach hook . One "PreToolUse", "PostToolUse", "UserPromptSubmit", "Stop", "SubagentStop". hook multi-hook repositories store scripts hooks/ subdirectory, name hook install (without file extension), e.g. hook = "lint-staged". supplied, script read hooks/<hook>.<ext> within repository. Ignored source already points specific file. matcher optional tool-name pattern ("PreToolUse" "PostToolUse" events) used filter tool calls trigger hook, e.g. "Bash|Edit". NULL (default), hook applies tool calls event. path hooks directory. Can one : known coding agent name 'claude_code', 'cursor', 'github_copilot' (see hook_path() full list) use agent's conventional project-scope path. character string giving directory path directly. NULL (default), case path resolved WF_AGENT environment variable, prompting interactive sessions. Set WF_AGENT .Renviron (e.g. usethis::edit_r_environ()) avoid prompt. settings Path settings.json file hook registered. NULL (default), defaults settings.json parent directory path (e.g. path .claude/hooks, uses .claude/settings.json). overwrite FALSE (default), error raised hook already installed. Set TRUE replace . timeout Optional timeout seconds hook command. async TRUE, hook runs asynchronously. Default FALSE.

title: Install a hook script — add_hook
text: path installed hook script, invisibly.

title: Install a hook script — add_hook
code: tmp_hook <- tempfile(fileext = '.sh') writeLines(c('#!/bin/bash', 'echo hello'), tmp_hook) tmp_dir <- tempfile() tmp_settings <- tempfile(fileext = '.json') add_hook(tmp_hook,   event = 'PreToolUse', path = tmp_dir,   settings = tmp_settings ) #> Registered PreToolUse hook in /tmp/Rtmp1p0v0x/file1be560b6bb66.json. #> Installed hook "file1be52472354c" to #> /tmp/Rtmp1p0v0x/file1be568d31454/file1be52472354c.sh.

title: Install a rule — add_rule
text: Installs rule GitHub repository local file rules directory. Rules Markdown files optional YAML frontmatter. rule name comes frontmatter present, otherwise filename stem.

title: Install a rule — add_rule
code: add_rule(source, rule = NULL, path = NULL, overwrite = FALSE)

title: Install a rule — add_rule
text: source One : GitHub URL pointing repo, e.g. "https://github.com/owner/repo". GitHub URL pointing subdirectory file, e.g. "https://github.com/owner/repo/tree/main/path//rule.md". GitHub shorthand, e.g. "owner/repo". local file path pointing Markdown file. rule multi-rule repositories store rules rules/ subdirectory, name rule install (without .md extension), e.g. rule = "testing". supplied, rule read rules/<rule>.md within repository. Ignored source already points specific path via /tree/.... path rules directory. Can one : known coding agent name 'claude_code', 'cursor', 'github_copilot' (see rule_path() full list) use agent's conventional project-scope path. character string giving directory path directly. NULL (default), case path resolved WF_AGENT environment variable, prompting interactive sessions. Set WF_AGENT .Renviron (e.g. usethis::edit_r_environ()) avoid prompt. overwrite FALSE (default), error raised rule already installed. Set TRUE replace .

title: Install a rule — add_rule
text: path installed rule file, invisibly.

title: Install a rule — add_rule
code: src <- tempfile(fileext = '.md') writeLines(   c('---', 'name: example', 'description: An example rule.', '---'),   src ) add_rule(src, path = tempfile()) #> Installed rule "example" to /tmp/Rtmp1p0v0x/file1be54023fa66/example.md.

title: Install a skill — add_skill
text: Installs skill GitHub repository local directory skills directory.

title: Install a skill — add_skill
code: add_skill(source, skill = NULL, path = NULL, overwrite = FALSE)

title: Install a skill — add_skill
text: source One : GitHub URL pointing repo, e.g. "https://github.com/owner/repo". GitHub URL pointing subdirectory, e.g. "https://github.com/owner/repo/tree/main/path//skill". GitHub shorthand, e.g. "owner/repo". local directory path containing SKILL.md file. skill multi-skill repositories store skills skills/ subdirectory, name skill install, e.g. skill = "proofread". supplied, skill read skills/<skill> within repository. Ignored source already points specific subdirectory via /tree/.... path skills directory. Can one : known coding agent name 'claude_code', 'cursor', 'github_copilot' (see skill_path() full list) use agent's conventional project-scope path. character string giving directory path directly. NULL (default), case path resolved WF_AGENT environment variable, prompting interactive sessions. Set WF_AGENT .Renviron (e.g. usethis::edit_r_environ()) avoid prompt. overwrite FALSE (default), error raised skill already installed. Set TRUE replace .

title: Install a skill — add_skill
text: path installed skill directory, invisibly.

title: Install a skill — add_skill
code: src <- tempfile() dir.create(src) writeLines(   c('---', 'name: example', 'description: An example skill.', '---'),   file.path(src, 'SKILL.md') ) add_skill(src, path = tempfile()) #> Installed skill "example" to /tmp/Rtmp1p0v0x/file1be56a2584cc/example.

what: Get the conventional agent path for a coding agent — agent_path
title: Get the conventional agent path for a coding agent — agent_path
text: Returns conventional directory path custom agents given coding agent stored. path expanded (.e., ~ resolved home directory). Use fs::path_expand() need absolute path.

title: Get the conventional agent path for a coding agent — agent_path
code: agent_path(agent = NULL, scope = c("project", "global"))

title: Get the conventional agent path for a coding agent — agent_path
text: agent One "claude_code" (alias "claude"), "openclaw", "codex", "cursor", "gemini_cli", "github_copilot" (alias "copilot"), "posit_ai" (alias "posit"). NULL (default), agent resolved order: (1) WF_AGENT environment variable, (2) scan current working directory recognised agent config folder (.claude, .cursor, etc.), (3) final fallback "claude_code". Set WF_AGENT .Renviron (e.g. usethis::edit_r_environ()) avoid passing agent every time. scope One "project" (path relative current working directory, suitable committing version control) "global" (path user's home directory, available across projects).

title: Get the conventional agent path for a coding agent — agent_path
text: length-1 character vector giving conventional agents path.

title: Get the conventional agent path for a coding agent — agent_path
code: agent_path('claude_code', 'project') #> [1] ".claude/agents" agent_path('claude', 'project') # alias for claude_code #> [1] ".claude/agents" agent_path('cursor', 'global') #> [1] "~/.cursor/agents" agent_path() # auto-detects from WF_AGENT, dir scan, or falls back to claude_code #> [1] ".claude/agents"

what: Check installed agents for available updates — check_agents
title: Check installed agents for available updates — check_agents
text: Compares installed agent's recorded commit SHA latest commit GitHub. Local agents reported updatable.

title: Check installed agents for available updates — check_agents
code: check_agents(path = NULL)

title: Check installed agents for available updates — check_agents
text: path agents directory. Can one : known coding agent name 'claude_code', 'cursor', 'github_copilot' (see agent_path() full list) use agent's conventional project-scope path. character string giving directory path directly. NULL (default), case path resolved WF_AGENT environment variable, prompting interactive sessions. Set WF_AGENT .Renviron (e.g. usethis::edit_r_environ()) avoid prompt.

title: Check installed agents for available updates — check_agents
text: data frame columns: name: agent name. installed_sha: SHA recorded install time (NA local). latest_sha: current HEAD SHA GitHub (NA local network failure). update_available: TRUE installed latest SHAs differ.

title: Check installed agents for available updates — check_agents
code: check_agents(tempfile()) #>      name installed_sha latest_sha update_available #> 1 example          <NA>       <NA>            FALSE

what: Search for agents on GitHub — find_agent
title: Search for agents on GitHub — find_agent
text: Searches GitHub repositories tagged agent topic matching keyword query. Searches across supported agent topic conventions (e.g. claude-agent, cursor-agent).

title: Search for agents on GitHub — find_agent
code: find_agent(query)

title: Search for agents on GitHub — find_agent
text: query Keyword search .

title: Search for agents on GitHub — find_agent
text: data frame columns: name: repository name. description: repository description. owner: repository owner login. url: full URL repository. stars: number GitHub stars.

title: Search for agents on GitHub — find_agent
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Requires GitHub API access; may fail due to rate limiting find_agent('code-review') } # }

what: Create a new agent template — init_agent
title: Create a new agent template — init_agent
text: Creates new agent file path/name.md containing template ready filled .

title: Create a new agent template — init_agent
code: init_agent(name, path = NULL)

title: Create a new agent template — init_agent
text: name Agent name. Must 1-64 characters, lowercase alphanumeric single hyphens (consecutive --), start end hyphen. Consider using gerund role form (e.g. "code-reviewer", "test-writer"). path Directory create agent file. Can one : known coding agent name "claude_code" "github_copilot" use agent's conventional project-scope path (see agent_path() full list). character string giving directory path directly. NULL (default), case path resolved WF_AGENT environment variable, prompting interactive sessions. Set WF_AGENT .Renviron (e.g. usethis::edit_r_environ()) avoid prompt.

title: Create a new agent template — init_agent
text: path new agent file, invisibly.

title: Create a new agent template — init_agent
code: init_agent('my-agent', tempfile()) #> Created agent "my-agent" at /tmp/Rtmp1p0v0x/file1be5793fbb34/my-agent.md.

what: Create a new hook template — init_hook
title: Create a new hook template — init_hook
text: Creates new shell script hook file path/name.sh containing minimal template ready filled . editing script, register register_hook() install add_hook().

title: Create a new hook template — init_hook
code: init_hook(name, path = NULL)

title: Create a new hook template — init_hook
text: name Hook name. Must 1-64 characters, lowercase alphanumeric single hyphens (consecutive --), start end hyphen. Consider using kebab-case verb form (e.g. "lint--save", "check-format"). path Directory create hook file. Can one : known coding agent name "claude_code" "github_copilot" use agent's conventional project-scope path (see hook_path() full list). character string giving directory path directly. NULL (default), case path resolved WF_AGENT environment variable, prompting interactive sessions. Set WF_AGENT .Renviron (e.g. usethis::edit_r_environ()) avoid prompt.

title: Create a new hook template — init_hook
text: path new hook file, invisibly.

title: Create a new rule template — init_rule
text: name Rule name. Must 1-64 characters, lowercase alphanumeric single hyphens (consecutive --), start end hyphen. Consider descriptive noun form (e.g. "testing", "code-style"). path Directory create rule file. Can one : known coding agent name "claude_code" "github_copilot" use agent's conventional project-scope path (see rule_path() full list). character string giving directory path directly. NULL (default), case path resolved WF_AGENT environment variable, prompting interactive sessions. Set WF_AGENT .Renviron (e.g. usethis::edit_r_environ()) avoid prompt.

title: Create a new skill template — init_skill
text: name Skill name. Must 1-64 characters, lowercase alphanumeric single hyphens (consecutive --), start end hyphen. Consider using gerund form (e.g. "parsing-logs"). path Directory create skill. skill directory path/name. Can one : known agent name "claude_code" "github_copilot" use agent's conventional project-scope path (see skill_path() full list). character string giving directory path directly. NULL (default), case path resolved WF_AGENT environment variable, prompting interactive sessions. Set WF_AGENT .Renviron (e.g. usethis::edit_r_environ()) avoid prompt.

what: Register a hook command in the settings file — register_hook
title: Register a hook command in the settings file — register_hook
text: Adds shell command hook coding agent's settings.json file. Hooks run automatically agent events "PreToolUse" "UserPromptSubmit". install hook script GitHub local file, use add_hook() instead.

title: Register a hook command in the settings file — register_hook
code: register_hook(   event,   command,   matcher = NULL,   timeout = NULL,   async = FALSE,   agent = NULL,   scope = c("project", "local", "global"),   path = NULL )

title: Register a hook command in the settings file — register_hook
text: event lifecycle event attach hook . One "PreToolUse", "PostToolUse", "UserPromptSubmit", "Stop", "SubagentStop". command shell command execute hook fires. matcher optional tool-name pattern ("PreToolUse" "PostToolUse" events) used filter tool calls trigger hook, e.g. "Bash|Edit". NULL (default), hook applies tool calls event. timeout Optional timeout seconds hook command. async TRUE, hook runs asynchronously agent wait complete. Default FALSE. agent, scope, path Passed settings_path() locate settings file. Defaults resolve project-scope settings detected coding agent.

title: Register a hook command in the settings file — register_hook
text: path modified settings file, invisibly.

title: Register a hook command in the settings file — register_hook
code: tmp <- tempfile(fileext = '.json') register_hook('PreToolUse', 'echo hello', path = tmp) #> Registered PreToolUse hook in /tmp/Rtmp1p0v0x/file1be522de2651.json.

title: Remove an installed agent — remove_agent
text: Deletes agent file agents directory removes lock file.

title: Remove an installed agent — remove_agent
code: remove_agent(name, path = NULL, force = FALSE)

title: Remove an installed agent — remove_agent
text: name name agent remove (without .md extension). path agents directory. Can one : known coding agent name 'claude_code', 'cursor', 'github_copilot' (see agent_path() full list) use agent's conventional project-scope path. character string giving directory path directly. NULL (default), case path resolved WF_AGENT environment variable, prompting interactive sessions. Set WF_AGENT .Renviron (e.g. usethis::edit_r_environ()) avoid prompt. force FALSE (default), prompts confirmation interactive sessions. Set TRUE skip prompt.

title: Remove an installed agent — remove_agent
text: name removed agent, invisibly.

title: Remove an installed agent — remove_agent
code: src <- tempfile(fileext = '.md') writeLines(   c('---', 'name: example', 'description: An example agent.', '---'),   src ) tmp <- tempfile() add_agent(src, path = tmp) #> Installed agent "example" to /tmp/Rtmp1p0v0x/file1be52d0549e6/example.md. remove_agent('example', tmp, force = TRUE) #> Removed agent "example" from /tmp/Rtmp1p0v0x/file1be52d0549e6.

title: Remove an installed hook — remove_hook
text: Deletes hook script hooks directory, removes registration settings.json, removes lock file.

title: Remove an installed hook — remove_hook
code: remove_hook(name, path = NULL, settings = NULL, force = FALSE)

title: Remove an installed hook — remove_hook
text: name name hook remove (script filename stem, e.g. "lint-staged" lint-staged.sh). path hooks directory. Can one : known coding agent name 'claude_code', 'cursor', 'github_copilot' (see hook_path() full list) use agent's conventional project-scope path. character string giving directory path directly. NULL (default), case path resolved WF_AGENT environment variable, prompting interactive sessions. Set WF_AGENT .Renviron (e.g. usethis::edit_r_environ()) avoid prompt. settings Path settings.json file hook registered. NULL (default), defaults settings.json parent directory path. force FALSE (default), prompts confirmation interactive sessions. Set TRUE skip prompt.

title: Remove an installed hook — remove_hook
text: name removed hook, invisibly.

title: Remove an installed hook — remove_hook
code: tmp_hook <- tempfile(fileext = '.sh') writeLines(c('#!/bin/bash', 'echo hello'), tmp_hook) tmp_dir <- tempfile() tmp_settings <- tempfile(fileext = '.json') add_hook(tmp_hook,   event = 'PreToolUse', path = tmp_dir,   settings = tmp_settings ) #> Registered PreToolUse hook in /tmp/Rtmp1p0v0x/file1be51a7e94c8.json. #> Installed hook "file1be51a7f4625" to #> /tmp/Rtmp1p0v0x/file1be51f5ae638/file1be51a7f4625.sh. remove_hook(fs::path_ext_remove(basename(tmp_hook)), tmp_dir,   settings = tmp_settings, force = TRUE ) #> Removed hook "file1be51a7f4625" from /tmp/Rtmp1p0v0x/file1be51f5ae638.

what: Get the conventional rule path for a coding agent — rule_path
title: Get the conventional rule path for a coding agent — rule_path
text: Returns conventional directory path rules given coding agent stored. path expanded (.e., ~ resolved home directory). Use fs::path_expand() need absolute path.

title: Get the conventional rule path for a coding agent — rule_path
code: rule_path(agent = NULL, scope = c("project", "global"))

title: Get the conventional rule path for a coding agent — rule_path
text: agent One "claude_code" (alias "claude"), "openclaw", "codex", "cursor", "gemini_cli", "github_copilot" (alias "copilot"), "posit_ai" (alias "posit"). NULL (default), agent resolved order: (1) WF_AGENT environment variable, (2) scan current working directory recognised agent config folder (.claude, .cursor, etc.), (3) final fallback "claude_code". Set WF_AGENT .Renviron (e.g. usethis::edit_r_environ()) avoid passing agent every time. scope One "project" (path relative current working directory, suitable committing version control) "global" (path user's home directory, available across projects).

title: Get the conventional rule path for a coding agent — rule_path
text: length-1 character vector giving conventional rules path.

title: Get the conventional rule path for a coding agent — rule_path
code: rule_path('claude_code', 'project') #> [1] ".claude/rules" rule_path('claude', 'project') # alias for claude_code #> [1] ".claude/rules" rule_path('cursor', 'global') #> [1] "~/.cursor/rules" rule_path() # auto-detects from WF_AGENT, dir scan, or falls back to claude_code #> [1] ".claude/rules"

what: Get the path to a coding agent's settings file — settings_path
title: Get the path to a coding agent's settings file — settings_path
text: Returns path settings.json file hooks configured given coding agent scope. path expanded (.e., ~ resolved home directory). Use fs::path_expand() need absolute path.

title: Get the path to a coding agent's settings file — settings_path
code: settings_path(agent = NULL, scope = c("project", "local", "global"))

title: Get the path to a coding agent's settings file — settings_path
text: agent One "claude_code" (alias "claude"), "openclaw", "codex", "cursor", "gemini_cli", "github_copilot" (alias "copilot"), "posit_ai" (alias "posit"). NULL (default), agent resolved order: (1) WF_AGENT environment variable, (2) scan current working directory recognised agent config folder (.claude, .cursor, etc.), (3) final fallback "claude_code". Set WF_AGENT .Renviron (e.g. usethis::edit_r_environ()) avoid passing agent every time. scope One : "project": .{agent}/settings.json current directory. "local": .{agent}/settings.local.json (gitignored, personal overrides). "global": ~/.{agent}/settings.json.

title: Get the path to a coding agent's settings file — settings_path
text: length-1 character vector giving path settings file.

title: Get the path to a coding agent's settings file — settings_path
code: settings_path('claude_code', 'project') #> [1] ".claude/settings.json" settings_path('claude', 'local') # alias for claude_code #> [1] ".claude/settings.local.json" settings_path('cursor', 'global') #> [1] "~/.cursor/settings.json"

what: wf: Artificial Intelligence Workflow Tools — wf-package
title: wf: Artificial Intelligence Workflow Tools — wf-package
text: Manage skills large language model coding agents. Supports installing skills 'GitHub' local directories, tracking versions lock file, keeping installations current. Installations can scoped single project shared globally across projects.

title: wf: Artificial Intelligence Workflow Tools — wf-package
text: Maintainer: Christopher T. Kenny ctkenny@proton.(ORCID)

title: wf 0.1.0
text: installed components (skills, agents, hooks, rules) now tracked single .wf-lock.json file agent configuration root (e.g., .claude/) rather separate per-type lock files subdirectory. add_agent(), agent_path(), check_agents(), find_agent(), init_agent(), list_agents(), remove_agent(), update_agents() new functions managing custom subagents stored .{agent}/agents/. add_hook() new function installs hook script file GitHub local path agent’s hooks directory registers settings.json. check_hooks(), find_hook(), hook_path(), init_hook(), list_hooks(), remove_hook(), update_hooks() round hooks lifecycle, mirroring skills/agents/rules pattern. hook_path() returns hooks directory (e.g. .claude/hooks), settings file. register_hook() new function handles bare command registration settings.json without installing script file. settings_path() new function returns path coding agent’s settings.json. add_rule(), check_rules(), find_rule(), init_rule(), list_rules(), remove_rule(), rule_path(), update_rules() new functions managing coding rules stored .{agent}/rules/.

title: wf 0.0.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-19 add_skill(), check_skills(), find_skill(), init_skill(), list_skills(), remove_skill(), update_skills() new functions managing AI agent skills. add_skill(), check_skills(), list_skills(), remove_skill(), update_skills() now default path NULL. NULL, path resolved WF_AGENT environment variable, interactive prompt listing detected agent directories. Passing known agent name (e.g., "github_copilot") path now also supported shorthand agent’s conventional project-scope path. skill_path() now supports "copilot" alias "github_copilot". skill_path() now supports Posit AI ("posit_ai", alias "posit") base directory .positai.
